message:
Fix Sigma operator directory handling to load all rules (#5715)

## Summary

Fixed a bug in the Sigma operator's directory handling where only the
last file's rules were retained when processing directories containing
multiple rule files.

## Problem

The `monitor_state::update()` function had a critical issue:
- Called `std::exchange(rules, {})` on every recursive call when
processing directories
- This cleared the rules map before loading each file's rules
- Result: only the final file's rule was retained
- Additionally, directory handling returned early without executing the
comparison/logging logic

## Solution

Refactored the function into two parts:
- `update()` - Public entry point that:
  - Performs the exchange operation once at the start
- Handles all comparison and logging logic for added/updated/removed
rules
  
- `load_rules()` - Private helper that:
  - Recursively traverses files and directories
  - Loads rules without clearing the rules map
  - Allows all rules to accumulate properly

## Testing

- All existing sigma unit tests pass
- Manual testing confirms directories with multiple sigma rules now
correctly load all rules

## Files Changed

- `plugins/sigma/src/plugin.cpp` - Split update() into update() and
load_rules()

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,17 @@
+---
+title: Fix sigma operator directory handling to load all rules
+type: bugfix
+authors:
+  - mavam
+  - claude
+pr: 5715
+created: 2026-02-03T11:16:25.337105Z
+---
+
+The `sigma` operator now correctly loads all rules when given a directory containing multiple Sigma rule files. Previously, only the last processed rule file would be retained because the rules collection was being cleared on every recursive directory traversal.
+
+```tql
+sigma "/path/to/sigma/rules"
+```
+
+All rules found in the directory and its subdirectories will now be loaded and used to match against input events.
